description_full: alkemy is a multi-agent AI system that lets a single consulting partner deeply steward 15–20 key accounts instead of 3–5 — by autonomously running the precipitation chain (signal → person → hook) across each account, detecting burning-platform moments, and generating partner-grade meeting prep — and we're using Agent Simulation, Observability, and Optimizer to harden it against the conflicting-signal edge cases that break single-agent systems.*The Business Use CaseConsulting is a $500B+ industry built on partner relationships. We interviewed 23+ senior partners across Roland Berger, Oliver Wyman, and boutique firms over the past 8 months. The universal pattern:Partners deeply steward 3–5 key accounts at a time because the cognitive load of tracking signals, people, and pitch contexts saturates their attention.With the right instrumentation, the same partner could steward 15–20 accountsEvery AI tool on the market today is an analyst tool (research, market maps, decks). ChatGPT is commoditizing that layer.The partner layer does not exist as a product. Signals that matter this week. Prep for Tuesday's buyer meeting. The burning-platform moment in an account you already know. No product here.The Multi-Agent Architecturealkemy's Key Account Agent is a collaboration of six specialized agents orchestrated via the Agent Development Kit (ADK):1. Signal Agent — monitors external sources (news, earnings, SEC filings, LinkedIn, specialized trade press) for events affecting watched accounts.2. Interaction Agent — ingests the partner's internal context (uploaded call recordings, meeting notes, CRM exports) via MCP connectors to Fathom / Granola / Gong / Salesforce / Dynamics.3. Burning-Platform Synthesizer — detects the four-ingredient transformation-moment signature: (a) change/urgency at the client, (b) a problem the consultant can name, (c) external evidence of competitors moving ahead, (d) internal evidence the client can't solve alone.4. VP-Prep Packet Agent — generates the pre-meeting brief with buyer bio, career history, company's stated strategy, new-CEO/CFO signals, and burning issues pulled from analyst calls.5. Hero-Moment Agent — drafts the pitch framing that makes the buyer look like a hero to their boss.6. Orchestrator — prioritizes across accounts, routes tasks, surfaces the next-best-action to the partner each morning.All agents ground their outputs in cited evidence via Vertex AI Search over public sources + the firm's private IP. Nothing is surfaced without a source link.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveThe Problem Sukhi SolvesIn India, a patient’s medical history is a "digital disaster." Critical data is scattered across physical files, WhatsApp chats, and various lab portals (e.g., Apollo, Dr. Lal). When a patient visits a new doctor, this fragmentation leads to diagnoses based on incomplete data, redundant tests, and high cognitive load for both the patient and the physician.Our solutionSolution & VisionSukhi acts as a Unified Health Intelligence Layer. It doesn't just store PDFs; it aggregates data from multiple sources to create a longitudinal health timeline. By converting "messy" data into structured biomarker trendlines, Sukhi empowers doctors with instant context and patients with proactive health roadmaps.

project_name: DocPod Enterprise: Accessible AI Learning Orchestrator
description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveReading long documents is a friction point for learning, especially for students with ADHD or visual impairments. Existing text-to-speech tools often sound robotic and lack the conversational nuance needed for high knowledge retention. Busy professionals and students need a way to consume complex information passively but effectively.Our solutionDocPod is an agentic platform that uses Gemini 2.5 Flash to transform any text or URL into a professional, two-host AI podcast. Our multi-agent orchestration pipeline analyzes documents, extracts core insights, and generates dynamic dialogue between analytical and curious hosts. With specialized "Learning Personas" and MCP integration for library management, DocPod makes knowledge instantly accessible and engaging.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveMillions of students across Nigeria and Africa prepare for high stakes exams like JAMB and WAEC every year, but most of them study alone with textbooks, no feedback and no way of knowing if they actually understand what they are reading. They watch tutorial videos and move on without really knowing if the concept stuck. By the time they sit the exam, they discover too late that they had gaps in their understanding. Traditional exam prep is passive, one size fits all and completely blind to how each student is actually learning.Our solutionVarlc is an AI powered learning platform that makes exam preparation intelligent and personal. Instead of just watching videos and hoping for the best, students learn through expert tutorial videos that are interrupted at key moments by smart comprehension checks. The system analyzes how each student responds, how long they take, where they struggle, and uses that data to build a personalized study path that adapts in real time.We are building an AI agent that acts like a personal study coach. It watches how a student learns, identifies weak areas automatically, adjusts the difficulty of what comes next and delivers personalized recaps that focus on exactly what that student needs to review. The agent does not just respond to questions, it proactively guides the student toward exam readiness without them having to figure out what to study next.

Problem to solveSoftware teams and startups often lose time between idea and execution. A client shares requirements in text or PDF form, but turning that into a production-ready application still needs many manual steps: understanding the scope, choosing the architecture, selecting the stack, planning environments, writing boilerplate, setting up CI/CD, preparing deployment, and coordinating decisions across development, DevOps, and QA. This slows delivery, creates inconsistency, and makes it harder for smaller teams to move fast with confidence.Our solutionSmartDev Agent is an AI-powered software delivery assistant that acts like a senior architect and technical co-pilot. It accepts a requirement as plain text or PDF, analyzes the project type and complexity, makes justified technical decisions, asks only the essential developer questions, and then scaffolds a production-oriented project flow end to end.The system includes:requirement parsing for text and PDF inputsintelligent project classification and architecture decision-makingguided developer questions for GitHub setup, environment variables, and hostingautomatic generation of backend/frontend starter code, Docker setup, environment files, tests, and CI/CD workflowsMCP-style integrations for file handling, GitHub planning, cloud deployment planning, and notificationsboth a CLI interface and a Streamlit dashboard for a smooth developer experienceOur goal is to reduce the gap between an idea and a deployable application. Instead of just generating code, SmartDev Agent helps structure the whole delivery workflow so developers can move from requirement to implementation much faster and with better technical consistency.

The Complex Business ProblemCurrent computer vision fitness tools rely on ancient models that flatten time and conflate root causes with downstream symptoms. A standard model can detect a symptom like "knee valgus," but it cannot tell a trainer if that valgus was caused by restricted ankle dorsiflexion or weak hip abductors. This creates a massive ceiling on AI utility in the B2B physical therapy and elite coaching markets. Detecting a symptom is a parlor trick; diagnosing the root cause is a highly monetizable clinical service. Elite trainers and studios need a way to scale their specific, gold-standard diagnostic reasoning across hundreds of clients without dilution. The Solution: A Per-Joint Specialist SwarmKinetic Swarm is net-new autonomous system using the the Agent Development Kit to break this problem apart. Instead of relying on a single black-box model to look at the whole body, we will build a "Kinetic Chain Swarm". We deploy parallel, isolated agents. Each agent is responsible for a single joint in the kinetic chain. Operating on declarative biomechanical ontologies engineered into their system prompts, these agents act as highly focused specialists. A coordinator Agent then arbitrates their parallel outputs to resolve the temporal sequence of a movement fault. If the Ankle Agent flags a range-of-motion limit at frame 138, and the Knee Agent flags valgus at frame 142, the Coordinator definitively diagnoses the ankle as the casual driver. Moving the Needle for Kinetic SwarmThis architecture solves the core defensibility problem in the $40Billion movement health market. By separating the pattern recognition (weights) from the clinical knowledge (YAML ontologies acting as structured prompts), we allow gyms, physical therapists, and elite trainers to audit, edit, and scale their exact coaching standards. It transforms subjective movement assessment into deterministic, liability-reducing documentation.

track: Refactor for Google Cloud Marketplace & Gemini Enterprise

We abstract AI system design.”

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Description: RecruitAgent is an autonomous multi-agent system that manages the full recruitment lifecycle. Drop in a job requirement and watch 5 specialized AI agents autonomously find candidates, draft personalized outreach, screen responses, and prepare submission packages — turning days of recruiter work into minutes.Problem to solveRecruiters spend 80% of their time on repetitive manual tasks — searching for candidates across LinkedIn, job boards, and databases, writing outreach emails one by one, screening responses, and preparingsubmission documents. The average time-to-fill is 44 days and cost-per-hire is $4,700. This fragmented workflow across 8-12 different tools leads to missed candidates, slow response times, inconsistent quality,and recruiter burnout. Current AI tools act as assistants that help recruiters work — but the recruiter still does the work. Our solutionRecruitAgent is an autonomous multi-agent system built on Google ADK and Gemini that handles the entire recruitment lifecycle without manual intervention. Drop in a job requirement and 5 specialized agents takeover — the Sourcing Agent finds matching candidates via Google Search, the Outreach Agent drafts and sends personalized emails through Gmail, the Screening Agent evaluates candidate responses and produces scored assessments, and the Submission Agent prepares a ready-to-review pipeline tracker in Google Sheets. Each agent connects to real-world tools via MCP (Model Context Protocol), producing tangible outputs — real emails sent, real spreadsheets created, scored candidate shortlists delivered to hiring managers. What takes a recruiter days, RecruitAgent completes in minutes.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveThe "Stateless Tutoring" Gap Current educational AI is "stateless," meaning it treats every interaction as a first-time meeting. Even if a student uses an AI tutor for three years, the AI "forgets" their specific learning hurdles, emotional triggers, and past conceptual gaps the moment the chat window closes. This lack of long-term continuity prevents AI from becoming a true mentor, forcing students to repeatedly explain their background and leaving long-term learning deficits (like a 9th-grade algebra weakness) to haunt them in 11th-grade calculus.Our solutionWe are building a persistent Study Agent that leverages the Gemini Enterprise Agent Memory Bank to act as a student’s "external cognitive hard drive." Unlike standard chatbots, this agent utilizes a graph-based memory architecture to track a student’s academic evolution over their entire school career.

project_name: Zovack: AI Agents for WhatsApp Economy
description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveOver 200 million businesses worldwide use WhatsApp to serve their customers, yet most are still replying manually, one message at a time. When the owner is unavailable, sales are lost. A single unanswered message can cost a customer forever.Our solutionZovack is an AI Agent platform built on the WhatsApp Business API that deploys autonomous agents for SMBs handling orders, bookings, payments, and customer conversations 24/7 across healthcare, e-commerce, restaurants, services, and transportation. We are optimizing our agents for production-grade reliability across 69+ countries and 6 industries using Google ADK and Gemini.

Problem to solveAquaculture operations are managed through manual monitoring and fixed decision rules. Operators watch sensor readings, respond to alerts, and adjust feeding or aeration based on experience and intuition. There is no system that can model farm conditions before changes are made, maintain a structured and queryable record of what is happening, or make autonomous decisions grounded in both historical state and simulated outcomes.The result is that interventions happen after problems have already surfaced. There is no way to test a change before applying it to a live system. Every decision is made in isolation, with no unified loop connecting simulation, state tracking, and automated control.Our solutionTrident is an AI agent system that manages aquaculture operations through three connected components working as a single control loop.Trident-Sandbox is the simulation environment. It models farm conditions including water quality, dissolved oxygen, ammonia buildup, fish biomass, feeding schedules, and equipment behavior. Before any action is taken, Sandbox can model what happens next under different interventions — giving the system a way to test decisions without touching the live farm.Trident-Archive is the system record. It maintains a structured, timestamped timeline of sensor readings, simulated readings, feeding events, operator actions, AI actions, alerts, and outcomes. Archive is the source of truth that both Sandbox and Engine read from and write to.Trident-Engine is the AI agent. It reads current and historical state from Archive, identifies when the system is drifting from expected behavior, models possible interventions in Sandbox, and executes the action most likely to restore stable conditions. It then writes the result back into Archive so the system can compare prediction against outcome over time.Because the control loop — read state, model outcomes, act, record results — runs the same way in simulation and in production, Trident can be tested, validated, and improved without risk to the live farm.

A multi-agent system that runs the entire procurement RFQ workflow autonomouslyProblem to solveB2B procurement still runs on email. A single RFQ takes a few days of manual work — chasing quotes, comparing offers, and checking compliance — leading to slow decisions and missed savings.Our solutionProcureIQ deploys specialized agents — Buyer, Compliance, and Decision — that negotiate in parallel and deliver a vetted vendor recommendation in minutes. Procurement teams move from doing the work to approving the outcome.

project_name: Operator - AI Agents That Run Your E-Commerce Business
description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveAfrica's e-commerce market is one of the fastest-growing in the world, projected to reach $113 billion by 2029, growing at nearly 12% annually. Kenya alone is the continent's third-largest e-commerce market, with the sector expected to grow from $2.3 billion in 2024 to $3.8 billion by 2028. Over 60% of these transactions happen on mobile devices, and commerce increasingly lives on WhatsApp and Instagram.Yet the SMEs driving this growth are operating with no infrastructure. They receive 100–300 customer inquiries daily on WhatsApp and Instagram but cannot respond fast enough, losing sales to faster competitors. After a sale, operations collapse, orders get lost, stock is oversold, deliveries go untracked, and owners make restocking decisions with zero data. These businesses are losing revenue at every step of the customer journey, not because they lack customers, but because they lack the infrastructure to serve them.Our solutionOperator is a multi-agent AI system that automates the entire revenue operation of African e-commerce SMEs. Built on Google ADK with four specialized agents:Sofia (Sales Agent) handles all customer conversations on WhatsApp and Instagram — qualifying leads, closing sales, and processing M-Pesa payments in under 30 seconds, in English and Swahili.Gilfoyle (Inventory Agent) manages stock in real time, preventing overselling and alerting owners before items run out.Jared (Fulfilment Agent) coordinates order dispatch and proactively updates customers on delivery status. Richard (Intelligence Agent) ingests data from all agents and delivers weekly plain-language business intelligence to the owner on WhatsApp. Together, the four agents run the complete revenue operation — autonomously.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveThe Cost of Hyper-Connectivity In an era where artificial intelligence and technology are relentlessly optimized for maximum output and constant engagement, individuals face unprecedented levels of cognitive overload, burnout, and anxiety. The modern digital experience prioritizes acceleration over well-being, leaving a critical gap in the market for solutions that encourage deceleration, emotional grounding, and sustainable living.Our solutionSerenity Pulse is an autonomous wellness agent designed to serve as a digital anchor in a chaotic landscape. Rather than driving users to do more, Serenity Pulse empowers them to step back. By securely and privately integrating with a user’s personal data ecosystems—including emails and messaging platforms, the agent acts as a proactive buffer and mindful companion, prioritizing the user's holistic well-being over raw productivity.

Problem to SolveIn the post-COVID era, the global e-commerce landscape has undergone a massive transformation, evolving from a convenience channel into a dominant force in retail. By 2026, global e-commerce sales are expected to reach $7.4 trillion, contributing over 22% of total retail sales worldwide.India stands out as one of the fastest-growing markets, projected to reach $160–226 billion by 2026. This growth is fueled by rapid smartphone adoption, low-cost internet, UPI-based payments, and a young, digitally native population. A major disruptor within this ecosystem is quick commerce (q-commerce), expected to reach nearly $7 billion, with platforms promising delivery within 10–30 minutes.Core Problem: Lack of a Single Source of Truth (SSOT)As brands expand across multiple channels—marketplaces, quick commerce platforms, D2C websites, and social commerce—their data ecosystem becomes increasingly fragmented.Each platform operates independently with its own:Data structures and formatsAPIs and reporting systemsUpdate frequencies (real-time vs delayed)Metrics definitionsThis creates a scenario where critical business data—orders, inventory, ads, returns, pricing, logistics, and payouts—is scattered across disconnected systems.To manage this complexity, companies build multiple ERP systems, dashboards, and spreadsheets. They also hire teams of analysts, SQL developers, and data engineers whose primary responsibility often becomes data reconciliation rather than value creation.Key Challenges Faced by BusinessesInventory Inefficiencies: Lack of synchronization leads to overselling (causing cancellations and refunds) or stockouts (lost revenue and poor customer experience).Delayed Decision-Making: Data aggregation delays prevent real-time pricing, promotion, and demand optimization.High Operational Costs: Significant resources are spent on manual data cleaning and reporting.No Unified Business View: Absence of a 360° view across channels limits strategic planning and personalization.Scalability Issues: During peak demand (e.g., festive sales or quick commerce spikes), manual processes fail to keep up.Industry estimates suggest that businesses lose 5–15% of potential revenue due to these inefficiencies. In quick commerce, where speed is critical, even minor delays in data can result in poor inventory allocation and missed hyperlocal demand.Why Existing Solutions Fall ShortTraditional ERP and analytics solutions attempt to solve this problem but introduce new limitations:High implementation and maintenance costsRigid architectures that struggle with new integrationsSlow adaptation to frequent API and platform changesContinued dependence on technical teamsThis leads to a fragmented, expensive, and inefficient ecosystem—especially challenging for SMBs that lack large technical resources.Our Solution: UniSync AIUniSync AI is an intelligent, cloud-native platform designed to act as both a Single Source of Truth and a personal AI analytics team for e-commerce businesses.Instead of relying on multiple analysts and engineers, UniSync AI uses a multi-agent AI architecture powered by models from Google to automate data ingestion, standardization, analysis, and decision-making.Multi-Agent ArchitectureSupervisor Agent: Acts as the central brain, interpreting user queries, breaking them into tasks, coordinating agents, and delivering unified insights.Order Agent: Monitors order lifecycle, cancellations, returns, and fulfillment performance across platforms in real time.Inventory Agent: Maintains unified inventory visibility, predicts stockouts, prevents overselling, and optimizes replenishment—critical for quick commerce.Ads Agent: Evaluates campaign performance, tracks ROI, and recommends budget allocation and bidding strategies.PnL Agent: Builds real-time profitability views by combining revenue, ad spend, fees, logistics, and returns.RCA Agent: Performs root cause analysis for anomalies such as sales drops or high cancellations, providing clear explanations backed by data.How It WorksData from multiple platforms is continuously ingested and standardized into a unified layer built on DB, forming a reliable SSOT. AI models are powered by Gemini process this data in real time.The Supervisor Agent orchestrates responses across agents and delivers clear, actionable insights instantly.Key BenefitsEliminates dependency on large data and analytics teamsProvides real-time, accurate, and unified insightsEnables faster and smarter decision-makingReduces operational costs significantlyScales efficiently for both SMBs and enterprisesConclusionUniSync AI transforms fragmented, multi-channel e-commerce data into a centralized intelligence system. By replacing manual processes with autonomous AI agents, it empowers businesses to operate efficiently, improve profitability, and compete effectively in the rapidly evolving digital commerce ecosystem.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveRoad infrastructure deteriorates over time due to traffic load, weather conditions, and poor maintenance. This leads to multiple types of road distress such as potholes, cracks, surface wear, edge failures, and uneven pavements.Today, most road inspections are manual teams physically survey roads, which is time-consuming, expensive, and often inconsistent. As a result:Damage is detected lateSeverity is not accurately assessedMaintenance decisions are delayedThis directly impacts public safety, increases vehicle damage, and raises long-term repair costs. There is a clear need for a faster, more reliable, and scalable way to monitor road conditions.Our solutionWe are building an AI-powered Road Intelligence Agent that automates road condition monitoring and analysis.The system uses computer vision and intelligent agents to:Detect multiple types of road distress (potholes, cracks, surface degradation, uneven roads) using a YOLOv8 modelAnalyze the severity of each issue (low, medium, high) based on visual patternsProvide actionable recommendations for maintenance (e.g., patching, resurfacing, urgent repair)Generate structured reports that can assist authorities in prioritizing repairsBy combining machine learning with an agent-based approach, the system not only identifies problems but also interprets them and suggests next steps. This makes road maintenance more proactive, efficient, and scalable.

RealtyOps OS is an AI operating system for real-estate brokerage teams that turns inbound leads into scheduled property tours with speed, consistency, and trust. Most broker teams lose revenue because follow-up is manual, inconsistent, and delayed across channels. Leads come from website forms and ad campaigns, but response quality depends on agent availability. RealtyOps OS solves this by running a hybrid-autonomous workflow: it qualifies leads, initiates personalized outreach, handles follow-up cadence, negotiates available slots, syncs with HubSpot and Google Calendar, and books tours automatically when qualification criteria are met. For ambiguous or high-risk cases, it routes decisions to a human broker with full context. Built with Gemini and Google’s agent tooling, RealtyOps OS is designed as a channel-flexible system architecture rather than a single chatbot. In this version, it supports website and Meta lead intake, with extensible connectors for additional sources. The system enforces clear qualification gates (budget range, preferred locality, move-in timeline, verified contact), executes a 3-step follow-up cadence, and maintains an auditable action trail for operational trust. Our core business goal is measurable revenue impact. RealtyOps OS is benchmarked with an A/B simulation baseline and targets: - +15% tour-scheduled conversion rate - +25% follow-up completion rate - Under 3 minutes median lead response time RealtyOps OS helps broker teams move from ad spend to booked tours faster, while giving founders and sales leaders a reliable AI execution layer they can actually trust in production

Problem to SolveMulti-location small businesses and franchises in Latin America (convenience stores, hardware networks, grocery franchisees) are currently losing significant margins due to three critical friction points:The "Knowledge Gap" & High Turnover: Training a 19-year-old on their first job to master complex POS software, memorize thousands of stock prices, and understand business rules takes up to week of paid onboarding; 2 months later the employee leaves. In a high-turnover environment, this cost is a constant, unproductive drain on capital.Operational Chaos (The "Paper & WhatsApp" Trap): Cashiers currently juggle paper notebooks, informal WhatsApp pings to owners, and long Excel sheets to reconstruct daily cash flow. This disorganized workflow is highly prone to human error, creates trust gaps between owners and staff, and leaves headquarters with zero real-time visibility across stores.Prohibitive Scaling Costs: Traditional retail tech requires legacy point-of-sale (POS) hardware ($500–$1,500 per checkout) and per-seat software licenses. For a growing franchise, these capital expenditures (CAPEX) make scaling slow and financially risky.The result: Inventory drifts until it runs out mid-sale, owners can't trust their numbers, and businesses remain "blind" to their own operations. Our SolutionVelora is a B2B AI-native operating system for multi-location businesses, built 100% on the Google Cloud ecosystem. We replace legacy POS hardware with an intelligent multi-agent architecture that slashes training from weeks to minutes, and the employees cellphone becomes the buisiness terminal, saving costs by a big margin.The Architecture: Google-Native IntelligenceEmployee Agent (Front-Line Ops): Built with Gemini 2.5 Flash and orchestrated via the Google ADK (Agent Development Kit). It acts as the cashier's "shift partner."Supervisor Agent (Strategic Oversight): Powered by Gemini 2.5 Pro on Vertex AI Agent Engine. A2A (Agent-to-Agent) Protocol running over Cloud Pub/Sub.And much more...Visit www.somosvelora.com for more info

Powered by Gemini.

Launching a product is not a single task, it’s a complex, multi-step process involving decision-making, planning, coordination, and execution across different roles like marketing, finance, and operations.Today’s AI agents can assist with isolated tasks, writing content, generating plans, or answering questions but they fail when faced with real-world complexity:They break in multi-step workflowsThey cannot handle conflicting constraints (budget vs timeline vs quality)They lack memory and coordination across rolesThey stall or produce inconsistent outputs when conditions changeIn reality, launching a product requires continuous adaptation, not static outputs.👉 The core problem is not intelligence.👉 The real problem is reliability in execution under dynamic conditions.Our SolutionRivtor: Autonomous Company Engine is a multi-agent system designed to simulate and execute real business workflows end-to-end from decision to planning to execution, while handling real-world uncertainty.For this project, we focus on a single high-stakes workflow:👉 Launching a product autonomouslyRivtor is composed of specialized agents (e.g., Marketing, Finance, Operations) that:Interpret goals and make strategic decisionsBreak down plans into structured, interdependent tasksExecute actions (content creation, scheduling, task triggering, etc.)Continuously monitor constraints like budget, time, and dependenciesDetect failures and conflicts during executionAdapt and re-plan automatically to recover and continueUnlike traditional agents, Rivtor does not stop at generating outputs.It operates as a closed-loop system:Decision → Planning → Execution → Failure Detection → Recovery → ContinuationWhat Makes Rivtor DifferentEnd-to-End ExecutionGoes beyond recommendations—Rivtor actually carries out workflows.Multi-Agent CoordinationSimulates real organizational roles that collaborate and challenge each other.Failure-Aware SystemDesigned to detect when plans break (budget overflow, timeline conflicts, missing inputs).Self-Correcting LoopAutomatically adjusts decisions and plans without human intervention.Observability & TransparencyEvery decision, conflict, and correction is traceable, making the system debuggable and reliable.Why It MattersMost AI systems today are assistants.Rivtor represents a shift toward:👉 Autonomous execution systems that can operate in real-world environmentsBy focusing on reliability under complex, dynamic conditions, Rivtor addresses one of the biggest unsolved problems in AI agents: making them actually usable for real business operations.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveMany students struggle to understand complex topics because most learning platforms and AI assistants provide only text-based explanations instead of visual and structured teaching. Learners often need diagrams, step-by-step guidance, real-world examples, and personalized explanations based on their pace and understanding level. Existing AI chatbots act as single agents and do not collaborate to create an interactive teaching experience. There is a need for a smarter learning assistant that explains concepts visually and adaptively like a real tutor.Our solutionEduAgents is a multi-agent AI-powered learning assistant that helps students understand complex topics through conversation and visual explanations. Instead of using a single chatbot, EduAgents coordinates multiple specialized agents such as a Planner Agent, Teacher Agent, Visual Agent, Example Agent, and Quiz Agent to work together like a virtual teaching team. Students can ask questions naturally, and the system responds with structured explanations, diagrams, real-world examples, and quick assessments. This creates a personalized and interactive learning experience that makes difficult concepts easier to understand, especially for visual learners.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveProduction and on-site teams understand real operational problems, but they often struggle to explain them in structured IT/SAD terms. This causes unclear requirements, wrong assumptions, rework, and delays between business users and development teams.Our solutionSADify is an AI system analyst agent that turns messy business requirements into clarified, complete, developer-ready System Analysis and Design documents. It asks follow-up questions, checks completeness, highlights missing information, and generates structured SAD outputs for IT teams.

CausalMind Ops Engine- Detect, diagnose, patch, prove — self-healing pipelines for the modern data stack.Problem to solveCurrent tools stop at detection or, at best, root-cause localization - naming the failing service, table, or deploy. None close the loop: writing the patch, validating recovery against the business KPI, and signing an audit record. The senior data engineer still does that work at 3 AM.. Datadog detects a pipeline failed at 03:00 UTC, but cannot tell you that an upstream vendor renamed customer_status to status at 02:14 UTC and the fix is a four-line transformation patch. Monte Carlo flags the data anomaly but cannot write the SQL, regression test, or data contract. Sweep and Cursor agents wait for a human to type "fix this." Runbook automation runs deterministic playbooks for problems already understood. Every layer alerts or detects; none deploys a verified fix and turns the incident into a guardrail.This creates a massive ceiling on AI utility in B2B production reliability. Detecting that something broke is a parlor trick; closing the loop autonomously — with proven recovery and zero engineer minutes at 3 AM — is a highly monetizable enterprise service. The painful work of connecting an upstream vendor change to the downstream pipeline failure to the CFO's broken Monday dashboard, inferring the silently-shifted value mapping, generating the patch, validating recovery, and signing the SOX audit record still falls to the senior data engineer on call. Her reasoning stays locked inside her head, unscalable across thousands of pipelines.Mid-market data teams need a way to scale their gold-standard reliability reasoning without dilution. The market is large and underserved: AIOps is a $40B category and data contracts grow from $1.85B in 2024 to $8.72B by 2033 (Acceldata). Today's tools sit on the alert-and-detect side; the close-the-loop side is wide open.Our solutionCausalMind is a net-new autonomous reliability platform using the Agent Development Kit. Instead of stitching together monitoring, observability, code agents, and runbooks, we deploy a 12-agent specialist swarm that closes the loop end to end.A Coordinator — an ADK Custom Agent — orchestrates eleven specialists: Data, Log, Infra, API, Code Repair, Test, Validation, Learning, Governance, Deployment, and Cost. Each carries a narrow declarative system prompt and typed MCP toolset. Operating on declarative reliability ontologies — version-controlled data contracts, autonomy-tier policies, runbook RAG — they reason on customer-specific standards, not generic heuristics. Validation runs on a deliberately different model from Code Repair: a generator never grades its own work. The Coordinator runs the swarm in parallel for diagnosis, sequentially for remediation, and arbitrates outputs into an explicit causal graph. If Data flags a column rename in raw.customers at 02:14 UTC and Log flags a ColumnNotFound cluster at 02:31 UTC, the Coordinator diagnoses the vendor change as causal driver, generates the SQL patch, opens a GitHub PR under confidence-tiered autonomy, deploys after one human approval, validates the BigQuery dashboard recovers, and stores the signature so the next occurrence heals in seconds.Four capabilities differentiate CausalMind.Causal Lineage Diagnosis: reasoning over an explicit causal graph (APIs, BigQuery tables, Dataform actions, deployments, KPIs) instead of correlating log keywords.Drift Forecast: the Memory Agent predicts incidents by clustering vendor changelog cadence and signature similarity, surfacing risk days before impact.Time-Travel Debug: every step writes an immutable event to an append-only timeline — replayable as compliance evidence, debug surface, and audit record in one artifact.Confidence-Tiered Autonomy: trust climbs from recommend-only, through PR with human merge, to auto-merge of low-risk patterns with 50+ prior successes.By separating agent reasoning (Gemini + ADK + MCP) from operational knowledge (contracts, autonomy policies, runbook RAG, timelines), data teams, CISOs, and CFOs audit, edit, and scale their exact reliability standards without dilution. Every fix carries a cryptographic provenance record signed at deploy — transforming subjective 3 AM triage into SOX-grade documentation. Reference customer Helio Finance, a mid-market fintech, spends ~$416K/year on drift triage; CausalMind targets 80% elimination at $48K/year — a 7× year-one return.Powered by Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform end to end. Deployed to Vertex AI Agent Runtime; Sessions hold per-incident state, Memory Bank manages long-term pattern similarity. Reasoning runs on Gemini 2.5 Pro and 2.0 Flash. Custom RAG grounds against Agent Search Data Stores (BigQuery-backed) for runbooks and contracts; Grounding with Google Search powers Drift Forecast by monitoring vendor changelogs. Discoverable via A2A at /.well-known/agent.json so other agents in a Gemini Enterprise mesh can query CausalMind in natural language.

Problem to solveBehind-the-wheel lessons should be learning experiences, not pass/fail moments or overwhelming correction lists.In a good drive lesson, the student is practicing skills and drills, learning as they go, and receiving simple follow-up from the instructor: “work on this next.” But in real-world instruction, two problems can happen. Some lessons drift toward evaluation-heavy instruction, where the student feels like every drive is a test. Other lessons end with too many corrections at once, leaving the student discouraged or unsure what actually matters most.Students need clear, respectful feedback they can digest. A young adult, international student, or student without strong practice support at home may need more than “brake earlier” or “watch your speed.” They need to understand the specific skill to work on, why it matters, and what practice step comes next.OpenDrive Clipboard helps licensed instructors turn teachable moments into instructor-reviewed debriefs that are simple, structured, and evidence-informed. A moment such as taking right turns too fast, missing signals, drifting into one-handed control, following too closely, or braking late can become a clear practice focus without turning the debrief into criticism or shame.The goal is not to replace instructor judgment. The goal is to help instructors preserve the lesson, connect observations to specific skills, and send students home with respectful next steps they can actually use.AI organizes the moment. The instructor leads the lesson.Our solutionOpenDrive Clipboard is a Gemini-powered, ADK-ready agent workflow that drafts post-drive debrief materials for a licensed instructor to review and approve.It runs after the lesson, not during the drive. It does not exist in the vehicle cabin. It does not capture interior audio. It does not control the vehicle, grade readiness, make licensing decisions, or communicate with the student during a drive.The agent takes a demo driving scenario, such as late braking near a crosswalk, residential speed creep, following too closely, missed signaling, or yellow-light indecision, and produces an instructor-ready draft containing:1. Safety summary2. Observed safety concern3. Suggested lesson focus4. Student reflection prompt5. Practice assignment6. Family-friendly summary7. Language-access preview8. Reviewed demo log entryEvery output is marked: DRAFT — INSTRUCTOR REVIEW REQUIRED.The draft is held at a hard human-review gate. The licensed instructor clicks Approve, Edit, Reject, or Regenerate. Nothing reaches a student or family without the instructor’s sign-off. This is the product’s safety boundary, regulatory boundary, and core differentiator.Architecture:- Gemini for reasoning and classification- Google ADK / ADK-ready orchestration- Typed tool layer for demo drive events, lesson-context retrieval, debrief drafting, language-access preview, and instructor review decisions- Vue 3 + Laravel instructor review-gate dashboard- Google Cloud Run deployment for the dashboard and agent servicesThe public demo uses demo data only: no real student records, no PII, no production OpenDrive Beacon data, no interior audio, and no production secrets in the public repo.Two products, one umbrella:OpenDrive Beacon is the private in-vehicle evidence layer: CAN bus, IMU, GPS, and forward-road camera data for future instructor-reviewed post-drive analysis.OpenDrive Clipboard is this public hackathon submission: the post-drive drafting and review layer that turns demo driving scenarios into instructor-reviewed debrief materials.Beacon records. Clipboard drafts. The licensed instructor decides.Regulatory posture:OpenDrive Clipboard supports licensed-instructor documentation and review. AI-assisted note drafting is treated as a documentation aid for the licensed professional, similar to a digital intake form, structured event log, or lesson-summary drafting aid.The system is not an AI driving instructor, vehicle-control system, licensing evaluator, diagnostic tool, enforcement tool, or readiness grader.OpenDrive™, OpenDrive Beacon™, and OpenDrive Clipboard™ are working names of Street Law OpenDrive.

MacroPilot AI – Autonomous Nutrition & Fitness Intelligence AgentOverviewMacroPilot AI is an autonomous, conversational health intelligence agent designed to help users track, optimize, and manage their nutrition and fitness in real time.Unlike traditional calorie trackers, MacroPilot goes beyond logging - it actively reasons over user behavior, adapts to goals, and executes multi-step workflows such as dietary analysis, macro optimization, and fitness recommendations.The system is built using an agentic architecture that integrates external tools, persistent memory, and structured workflows to deliver production-grade reliability.

Problem to solveProblem to solveSpanish accounting firms do not just struggle with reading invoices; they struggle with operating high-volume, multi-client invoice workflows across email, WhatsApp, drives, portals, and accounting systems. Manual batch review, tax coding, exception handling, missing-document follow-up, and tenant-by-tenant supervision make the process slow, expensive, difficult to scale, and prone to errors.The urgency is growing because Spain is moving toward mandatory B2B e-invoicing under Ley Crea y Crece, while VAT record books already have formal AEAT requirements and some taxpayers must manage VAT reporting through SII. As a result, firms need more than OCR: they need an operational layer that can review batches of invoices, monitor tenant health, detect anomalies, and keep document workflows under control with full traceability.Our solutionWe are building a production-grade AI backoffice agent for Spanish accounting firms on Google Cloud. Instead of using the LLM to read every invoice expensively, the agent uses our proprietary invoice-processing pipeline as a specialized low-cost perception layer, and combines it with Gemini 2.5 and the Agent Development Kit to orchestrate document intake, batch invoice review, tenant supervision, exception handling, and repetitive tax backoffice operations at scale.The agent is designed to supervise thousands of invoices across multiple tenants, not just process one document at a time. It can collect invoices from multiple channels, trigger bulk processing jobs, review low-confidence or inconsistent outputs, monitor invoice and tenant status across periods, flag anomalies, request missing documents, escalate edge cases to a human reviewer, and push validated results into the accounting workflow.

Problem to solveManaging a start-up requires many hats. Not only does a founder need to manage research and development, engineering, and marketing, they also need to focus on responding to customer queries, complaints or suggestions. This is a time-consuming task that often requires validating claims against internal databases, researching solutions and crafting suitable responses. Our solutionAn agent that will monitor incoming communication, categorise the type of message, validate any claims against internal logs and databases, determine a resolution, issue a refund where necessary, and communicate the resolution back to the customer.

An optimization agent that stress-tests developer-role matching, detects weak fit scores, and improves shortlist reliability using AI interview evidence.Problem to solveTechnology companies do not just need faster developer matching. They need matching they can trust.A candidate can look strong on a resume but fail in real technical reasoning. Another candidate can look average on paper but be a strong fit after reviewing GitHub, project history, salary expectations, timezone, and AI technical interview evidence.This creates a serious reliability problem for hiring agents. If the matching agent over-trusts resumes, ignores missing evidence, or fails on edge cases, the company receives weak-fit candidates with confident explanations. That creates false positives, wastes CTO and senior engineer time, and turns hiring back into a lottery.This is especially dangerous in technical hiring because the cost of a wrong engineering hire is high: months of lost development, salary, onboarding, team distraction, and another hiring cycle.The challenge is not only to match candidates faster. The challenge is to make the matching agent reliable under real-world edge cases.Our solutionWe are building WorkorAI Matching Reliability Agent — an optimization agent that improves the reliability of developer-role matching.The agent stress-tests WorkorAI matching logic against difficult hiring scenarios: incomplete resumes, inflated skill claims, missing salary data, timezone conflicts, mismatched seniority, strong GitHub but weak interview performance, or strong interview evidence but weak resume structure.It compares candidate claims from CV, GitHub, and LinkedIn against AI technical interview evidence and the role scorecard. Then it detects weak reasoning, flags unsupported fit scores, identifies missing evidence, and improves the final match report.The output is not just a ranked shortlist. It is a reliability-checked shortlist.Retrieval layer: RAG with gemini-embedding-003Each job and candidate profile is ingested as semantically chunked documents with explicit metadata boundaries for stack, seniority, locale, and AI interview transcript. Embeddings are generated using `gemini-embedding-003` and stored in a vector index. For each job↔candidate pair, vector search retrieves the most relevant evidence context and feeds it directly into the reasoning layer.Reasoning layer: Gemini 3 ProRetrieved evidence feeds a Gemini 3 Pro reasoning layer on top of WorkorAI's own matching model. It evaluates the full evidence picture — technical depth from GitHub, behavioral signals from the AI interview, stated expectations vs. role requirements — and produces a corrected, explainable match report.Each candidate report includes:— original fit score — corrected fit score — confidence level — missing evidence — risk notes — reasoning issues detected — AI interview evidence — recommended follow-up questionsDemo scenario:Before optimization, a candidate receives an 88% fit score because the resume lists Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and AWS.After the Matching Reliability Agent runs, the score is corrected to 71% because the AI technical interview shows weak system design reasoning, limited production AWS experience, and unclear ownership of backend architecture.The agent then explains the risks and generates follow-up interview questions for the CTO.ResultWorkorAI does not only match developers faster. It makes matching safer, more explainable, and more reliable for real hiring decisions.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveModern farming faces a perfect storm of pressures: unpredictable climate, depleted water tables, rising input costs, labor shortages, and volatile markets. Existing tools tackle small slices of this problem but rarely connect them.Pain PointsGeneric regional weather forecasts miss what is actually happening on a single field.Over-irrigation wastes up to 50% of agricultural water in many regions; under-irrigation kills yield.Disease and pest outbreaks are usually detected only after visible damage, when it is too late.Smallholder farmers cannot afford enterprise platforms like Climate FieldView or Taranis.Most digital tools are dashboards. They show data but do not make decisions or take action.Language and literacy barriers leave huge populations of farmers unable to use existing apps.Our solutionAgriMind is a cloud-hosted AI agent that pairs a reasoning engine with a library of specialized agronomy modules and a real-time alert system. It pulls live data from public weather APIs, optional on-farm sensors, and a built-in crop-knowledge database, then continuously asks itself: what is the best action for this field, this crop, this hour?The agent communicates with farmers through a lightweight mobile-first web app, SMS, WhatsApp, voice calls, or smart-speaker integration. When the agent has authority and connected hardware, it executes actions directly — opening an irrigation valve, dispatching a drone, or placing an input order. When it does not, it sends a clear alert with one-tap approval.

An MCP-powered hiring agent that lets company AI agents create hiring requests and receive verified developer matches for specific engineering roles.Problem to solveTechnology companies worldwide receive hundreds or even thousands of applications for engineering roles, but the best candidates often get lost among weak-fit profiles.For CTOs, founders, VP Engineering, and recruiting teams, hiring developers becomes like searching for a needle in a haystack. Teams manually review resumes, check tech stacks, assess experience, salary expectations, time zones, and technical depth.The cost of a wrong hire is high. When there is not enough verified data about a candidate, hiring becomes a lottery: decisions are made based on a resume, a short call, and a subjective impression. A bad engineering hire can cost months of lost development, salary, onboarding, team time, and another hiring cycle.Companies have already tried job boards, LinkedIn, ATS tools, recruiters, and AI agents. But these tools either increase the flow of profiles, store applications, or require manual communication. Company AI agents still do not have a secure way to create a hiring request and receive verified, explainable matching for a specific role.Our solutionWe are building WorkorAI Employer Hiring Agent — an MCP-powered agent interface for company AI agents.WorkorAI is a developer hiring platform that verifies candidates through CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and AI technical interviews, then matches them with engineering roles through explainable matching.An employer creates a workspace in WorkorAI, receives an API key, and connects it to their AI agent. After that, the agent can describe a role in natural language, send a hiring request through MCP, and receive a shortlist of developers who actually fit that role within minutes.Each match includes:— fit score — tech stack fit — salary fit — timezone fit — key match reasons — risks — evidence from the AI technical interview — questions for the next interview Demo scenarioA CTO writes to their AI agent:“Find me a senior backend engineer for a remote AI product team. We need Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS, product engineering experience, and availability in GMT+2–GMT+7.”Through WorkorAI Employer Hiring Agent, the agent creates a hiring request, sends the role description to WorkorAI, receives structured hiring intent, requests matched developers, and returns an explainable shortlist.Result: the company no longer searches for a needle in a haystack manually. Its AI agent gets verified matching for a specific engineering role within minutes.

project_name: Track3. WorkorAI Enterprise Self-Hosted Hiring Agent
track: Refactor for Google Cloud Marketplace & Gemini Enterprise
description_full: A self-hosted AI hiring system that runs inside the customer’s own Google Cloud environment, so candidate data, interview transcripts, and fit scores stay under the company’s control.Problem to solveEnterprise technical hiring has a data security problem.AI hiring tools can now screen, interview, and match developers at scale. But large companies cannot easily use third-party hiring SaaS when the workflow requires sending candidate CVs, AI interview transcripts, internal salary bands, role requirements, and fit scores into an external vendor environment.For legal, security, and compliance teams, this creates a hard blocker. Hiring data is sensitive: it includes candidate identity, technical performance, compensation expectations, workforce planning, and internal role requirements.The result is a bad tradeoff: enterprises either keep slow manual hiring workflows or use AI hiring tools while accepting sensitive data exposure.There is no simple enterprise-ready way to run an agent-native AI hiring system inside the company’s own infrastructure, on the company’s own keys, without routing sensitive hiring data through a third-party platform.Our solutionWe are building WorkorAI Enterprise Self-Hosted Hiring Agent — an AI hiring system that runs inside the customer’s own Google Cloud environment.The system verifies developers through CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and AI technical interviews, then matches them with engineering roles through explainable fit scoring. In the enterprise version, the entire workflow runs inside the customer’s perimeter.Instead of sending candidates to an external platform, the company invites them into its own private hiring instance. The AI interview, profile analysis, semantic matching, reliability scoring, match report, evidence, risks, and follow-up questions are generated locally.The system is designed for Google Cloud Marketplace and Gemini Enterprise:— deployed on Google Cloud via GKE or Cloud Run— Gemini 3 Pro runs on the customer’s own Google Cloud project and API keys— semantic matching uses gemini-embedding-003— A2A protocol enables discovery and coordination with enterprise agents— candidate data, interview transcripts, and fit scores stay inside the customer’s environment— audit trail supports legal, security, and compliance reviewDemo scenarioA company deploys the system inside its own Google Cloud project.Its internal HR agent sends a hiring request for a senior backend engineer. Candidates receive a direct link to the company’s private AI interviewer. The interview runs inside the company’s infrastructure. The matching agent processes CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and interview evidence locally.The CTO receives a verified shortlist with fit scores, evidence, risks, and follow-up questions — without candidate data ever leaving the company’s perimeter.ResultEnterprises get agent-native technical hiring without the data governance risk.Their cloud. Their keys. Their candidate data. Their hiring agents.

Ku-AI-Sales-System is a backend AI agent system that helps B2B sales teams find potential customers and write personalised cold outreach to them - automatically.A sales team starts by describing its product. From there, the system does three things:Finds leads — it searches directories like Crunchbase, G2, and LinkedIn to find companies that match the ideal customer profile.Researches each lead — it crawls the target company's website, social media, and news to build a detailed profile: who works there, who makes buying decisions, what tools they use, and what problems they might have.Writes the outreach — using everything it learned, it drafts two A/B-tested cold emails and a LinkedIn message for each decision maker, personalised to their role, interests, and pain points.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveExisting incident reviews rely on incomplete 5-Why chains, inconsistent action closure, and weak reuse of historical learning, which reduces RCA quality and delays prevention of repeat failures.Target Users:a) Reliability and maintenance teamsb) Safety and process excellence leadersOur solutionThis Application is an autonomous incident investigator and CAPA copilot for heavy metal industry operations that ingests CAPA records, cleans and normalizes incident data, retrieves similar historical cases through semantic similarity search, and generates structured RCA outputs through a LangGraph-orchestrated analysis pipeline. Its core value is to convert inconsistent manual investigations into repeatable, evidence-grounded outputs such as incident triage, executive summaries, timelines, 5-Whys, Fishbone, Bowtie, FMEA, Fault Tree Analysis, CAPA actions, and systemic recommendations.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveAI website builders are good at first drafts, but weak at real iteration. They regenerate too much, lose design consistency, and make section-level editing unreliable.Our solutionScytle is an AI design agent that generates and refines web pages on a structured canvas. Users can create full pages from a prompt, select any section, and update it in place without starting over.For this challenge, we’re entering the Optimize (Existing Agents) track and improving reliability across tool routing, edit precision, visual consistency, and production readiness.

project_name: Synapse: Shared memory for your AI agent fleet
team_names: Joseph Thomas; Tanishq Kaul; Marisha Boyd
description_full: Your AI agents have amnesia. Join the team curing it!Problem to solveAI agents are powerful but stateless. Every conversation starts from zero. They don't know what you watched last week, what your team decided in Tuesday's meeting, or which of your anchor concepts a new idea connects to. Meanwhile, knowledge workers ingest huge volumes of unstructured content (meetings, YouTube, docs, notes) that sit in silos and never get cross-referenced. The result: agents can't act as real thinking partners because they have no access to the context that makes you you. Giving every agent its own memory store doesn't scale either. What's missing is a shared, structured, queryable knowledge substrate that any agent can read from.Our solutionSynapse is a personal knowledge graph that functions as the shared memory layer for AI agent fleets. It ingests content from meetings, YouTube playlists, documents, and notes, then uses Gemini 2.5 Flash to extract typed entities and relationships across 24 categories, and Gemini text-embedding-004 to make every passage semantically retrievable. The graph is exposed to any MCP-compatible agent (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, custom runtimes) via 15+ tools including ask_synapse, search_entities, get_connections, and get_source_content.The loop compounds: every source you add makes every agent smarter, every query richer, and every cross-connection more likely to surface the non-obvious link that matters.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveEnterprises lose billions every year due to slow, manual, and error-prone procurement processes. Critical data is scattered across ERP systems, supplier portals, contracts, compliance databases, and market tools. Sourcing takes weeks, compliance risks are high, and human-dependent workflows lead to missed savings, stockouts, and costly mistakesOur solutionProcureGuard – a secure, multi-agent AI platform built natively on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It lets users simply describe what they need in plain English (“Source 500 laptops under budget with ESG compliance”). A team of specialized agents then autonomously researches suppliers, checks internal data, ensures compliance, negotiates, and generates purchase orders – all while staying fully governed and integrated with enterprise systems via MCP.Fast to build, production-ready, and designed to deliver 60-80% faster procurement cycles with measurable cost savings and near-zero compliance risk.

Problem to solve Standard AI chatbots degrade the educational process by instantly handing out answers instead of guiding a student's reasoning. Furthermore, learning is inherently visual and spatial, but current AI interactions are trapped in linear, text-based chat interfaces. Students don't just need an answer key; they need a dedicated mentor who can visually "see" their work, draw diagrams to explain complex concepts, and guide them through a structured, Socratic learning process without breaking conversational flow.Our solution We are building Tablo, a real-time, multimodal Socratic AI whiteboard. Tablo replaces the traditional chatbox with a shared digital canvas. Using a sub-second WebRTC voice loop paired with Gemini Live, the AI communicates via natural voice. Through a triggered board-snapshot system, the agent maintains spatial awareness of the student's work without exhausting context windows. The agent can natively execute deterministic commands to draw 20+ types of diagrams (SVGs, 3D shapes, math graphs) directly on the board alongside the student. Bounded by strict Socratic prompting, the agent acts as a true tutor. We will leverage Google Cloud infrastructure to transition Tablo to production-grade reliability, utilizing Gemini for core reasoning and multimodal RAG to securely ground the agent's knowledge in user-uploaded textbooks.

Problem to solveHealthcare education is often fragmented and inefficient for medical students. Students struggle to connect theoretical knowledge with clinical reasoning, lack personalized case-based practice, and spend excessive time searching multiple sources instead of learning in a structured way. There is no intelligent system that actively guides learning through adaptive questioning and clinical scenario simulation.Our solutionWe are building an autonomous AI medical learning agent that simulates real clinical reasoning sessions for medical students. The agent generates patient cases, analyzes user responses, asks adaptive follow-up questions, and provides step-by-step diagnostic reasoning feedback.It acts like a virtual clinical tutor:Generates dynamic medical casesEvaluates student decisions in real timeAdapts difficulty based on performanceExplains reasoning, not just answersTracks weak topics and reinforces themThe system is built using Gemini-powered agents and designed to operate as a multi-step autonomous learning assistant rather than a static Q&A tool.

Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveStartups get constant feedback from users across chats, tickets, calls and docs, but it sits scattered and unused.Founders and PMs waste hours manually reading, tagging and guessing what to build next, and still miss the real patterns and urgent issues.Our solutionAI that turns raw customer feedback into shippable product featuresCleo connects to your feedback sources, reads everything for you and groups similar pain points.It highlights what users need most right now and turns that into clear, ready-to-build feature ideas and tickets for your team. It can guide agentic tools and monitor the results and improvements in real time.

MentorIA Municipal Agents is a multi-agent orchestration system that turns the citizen-government interface into a single conversational entry point. Instead of forcing citizens to navigate fragmented portals, paper forms, and disconnected departments, our agents collaborate to interpret requests, validate documents, ground answers in municipal law, and route cases to the right department — all through WhatsApp or web chat in natural Spanish.Built on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Gemini 3.1 as the reasoning engine, the system orchestrates three specialized agents communicating via the A2A protocol: a Normative Agent grounded in municipal ordinances and Dominican constitutional law via Vertex AI Search, a Document Validator leveraging Gemini's native multimodal capabilities to verify IDs and permits, and a Routing Agent that connects to the municipality's case management system through a custom MCP server. The system is deployed on Cloud Run with Cloud Trace for observability, and produces an immutable audit trail for every decision.The pilot deployment targets the Ayuntamiento de La Romana under a signed Declaration of Interest with the municipal government. The architecture is designed to be replicated across Latin American municipalities through a "Digital Government Franchise" model promoted by IDECON 3x7, a Dominican civil society organization participating in the Google.org AI for Government Innovation Accelerator 2026 cohort.

project_name: AI-Powered Grant & Funding Discovery Agent for Startups
description_full: Problem to solveThe ProblemEarly-stage founders waste dozens of hours every month manually searching for grants, accelerators, competitions, and non-dilutive funding — across government portals, foundations, and platforms that update constantly. Most miss opportunities simply because they didn't know they existed.Our solutionAn autonomous multi-agent system where founders input a brief profile of their startup (sector, stage, location, team demographics), and the agent:Discovers relevant grants, competitions, and accelerators in real-time via MCP-connected tools (web search, government APIs, Crunchbase, etc.)Qualifies each opportunity against the startup's profile and eligibility criteriaDrafts tailored application sections using the founder's existing materialsTracks deadlines and sends proactive nudges when action is needed

TrialMatch is a multi-agent AI system that allows a single clinical research coordinator to deeply steward 100+ complex patient profiles across thousands of global trials—instead of 5–10—by autonomously running the recruitment chain (Profile → Search → Logic → Action). We use Agentic Workflows, Long-Context Reasoning, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to automate the high-stakes eligibility matching that currently causes 85% of clinical trials to fail their enrollment targets.The Business Use CaseClinical research is a $70B+ industry where the primary failure point is not science, but administrative latency. We analyzed the recruitment workflow at major oncology centers and identified a universal pattern:The Cognitive Saturation Point: Research nurses can only deeply steward a handful of patients at a time because the cognitive load of cross-referencing 100-page trial protocols against messy patient charts is a manual "search and compare" task.The "Analyst" Gap: Existing tools are static databases (search engines). They require the human to do the reading. There is no "Intelligence Layer" that reads the protocol for the nurse and justifies the match.The Cost of Silence: A single day of recruitment delay costs pharmaceutical sponsors between $600k and $8M in potential daily revenue. For the patient, it is the difference between a life-saving intervention and running out of time.

team_names: Darwin Thangappan; Evelyn James; Mohamed Imran; Mahiba Godwin; ARJUN PERIYASWAMY; Vicson Moses; Tom Praison
track: Refactor for Google Cloud Marketplace & Gemini Enterprise
description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveEnterprise users still spend too much time navigating complex ERP systems instead of simply expressing intent and getting work done.Our solutionACRA’s solution is an agentic AI platform built around a mesh of Enterprise AI Agents that work alongside humans as intelligent digital coworkers. Instead of forcing users to navigate complex ERP workflows and multiple enterprise systems manually, the platform enables users to simply express intent in natural language. The AI Agents understand the request, collaborate with other agents when needed, orchestrate actions across connected enterprise applications, and autonomously execute tasks on the user’s behalf. By combining AI-driven reasoning, workflow orchestration, and enterprise system integration, ACRA transforms traditional click-driven operations into outcome-driven autonomous execution.

project_name: KidTok - Lessons on Any Topic That Your Kid Loves to Finish
description_full: Problem to solveParents desperately want their children's screen time to matter, but the only alternative to low-quality YouTube is generic EdTech that doesn't hold a 4-year-old's attention for 30 seconds. There's no tool that lets a parent say "I want my child to learn curiosity about science, the way I am" and get back something their child will actually finish watching. The content that kids love (short-form video) and the content parents want them to consume (educational, values-aligned) have never overlapped - until now.My solutionKidTok is a multi-agent AI system that transforms any parent's intent into a fully produced, ~30-60-second personalized video lesson for their child in under 5 minutes. A parent describes a topic, their child's age, and what values they want to reinforce. An orchestrated pipeline of specialized agents, built on Google ADK and powered by Gemini, takes it from there:• A Curriculum Planner Agent scaffolds age-appropriate content using co-designed pedagogy (hook → learn → play → micro-reward), shaped by a 40-year kindergarten teacher.• A Content Agent generates narration lines, quiz moments, and values tagging via Gemini.• A Narration Agent calls ElevenLabs TTS via MCP tool connection.• A Visual Asset Agent generates scene-by-scene imagery.• A Video Composer Agent assembles the final personalized lesson.The result is not a YouTube recommendation, but a lesson built specifically for that child, by that parent's intent. KidTok is live in Beta and has paying customers since May 2, 2026.
